message:
fix(app,obligation): add double-panic guards to all Drop-based leak detectors

Four Drop impls previously used assert! or unconditional panic for leak
detection, which causes process abort if the thread is already unwinding
from another panic (double-panic). All four now:

1. Check the actual invariant condition with an if (not assert!)
2. Return early if std::thread::panicking() is true
3. Only then panic with the descriptive message

Affected types:
- AppHandle::drop: "APP HANDLE LEAKED" panic
- GradedObligation::drop: "OBLIGATION LEAKED" panic
- GradedScope::drop: "SCOPE LEAKED" panic
- ObligationToken<K>::drop: "OBLIGATION TOKEN LEAKED" panic

This makes unwinding safe in test harnesses and production scenarios
where a primary panic causes cascading drops of in-flight handles,
obligations, scopes, and tokens.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
